rationale: In the citing paper, Sprott et al. (2009) is used only as a psychometric benchmark when reporting that the BEA scale's test-retest reliability is comparable to prior studies. The citation appears in a grouped parenthetical with another reference and serves a generic support function rather than a distinct theoretical or empirical building block. The paper does not adopt the BESC construct/scale, derive hypotheses from it, or otherwise build a specific argument around that work.
